title: podman stats does not reflect resource constraints of container
body:
It appears that when running a container with resource limits like memory and cpu,
the limits are not being imposed on containers. podman stats does not reflect the limit.

Further checking cgroup slices in /sys filesystem, I see the cgroup slice for container 
does not have the limit set on them which was set during podman run.

version: podman-0.2.1-1.git3723cec.fc27.x86_64

Steps to reproduce:

- Run a container with resource constraints

`# podman run --cpu-shares 100 --memory 100m -d alpine sleep 10000 &`

```
# podman ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE    COMMAND       CREATED AT                      STATUS             PORTS   NAMES
22085e5fde9a   alpine   sleep 10000   2018-02-20 05:08:00 -0500 EST   Up 2 seconds ago           keen_borg
```

- Check output of `podman stats`
```
# podman stats --no-stream
ID             NAME        CPU %   MEM USAGE / LIMIT   MEM %   NET IO    BLOCK IO   PIDS
22085e5fde9a   keen_borg   --      1.47MB / 4.136GB    0.04%   -- / --   -- / --    2
```
- Check cgroup slice of container
```
# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-22085e5fde9acb7f2b82487f1f140da6919bfa539a1cb684d65232e90dfbeb1b/tasks
1950
1952

# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-22085e5fde9acb7f2b82487f1f140da6919bfa539a1cb684d65232e90dfbeb1b/cpu.shares
1024
```
```
# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-22085e5fde9acb7f2b82487f1f140da6919bfa539a1cb684d65232e90dfbeb1b/tasks
1950
1952

# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-22085e5fde9acb7f2b82487f1f140da6919bfa539a1cb684d65232e90dfbeb1b/memory.limit_in_bytes
9223372036854771712

# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-22085e5fde9acb7f2b82487f1f140da6919bfa539a1cb684d65232e90dfbeb1b/memory.usage_in_bytes
1470464
```

comments.body:
@sunilcio I started a container with the same run commands as you and the resulting container ID is bf32e0b00fdb41bf28c775bb374042e8dce6d60fc27e3f2885e8d61ed8cffe27.

If i look in /sys, i see:

```
 2018-02-21 08:34:11 ⌚  localhost in ~/tmp/keylabelvalue
○ → cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-bf32e0b00fdb41bf28c775bb374042e8dce6d60fc27e3f2885e8d61ed8cffe27/cpu.shares 
1024

 2018-02-21 08:36:16 ⌚  localhost in ~/tmp/keylabelvalue
○ → cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-bf32e0b00fdb41bf28c775bb374042e8dce6d60fc27e3f2885e8d61ed8cffe27/bf32e0b00fdb41bf28c775bb374042e8dce6d60fc27e3f2885e8d61ed8cffe27/cpu.shares
100
```

I cannot remember the reasoning for how this is done but I believe the subdir bf32.../cpu.shares are the actual ones to look at.  @mheon can you comment on this?

meanwhile i will look at the stat issue.

Adding what I further understood after discussion with @baude  ++ on irc.

In cgroup directory of the container, there is one more sub directory with 
container ID which actually contains the resource limits for the container.

If we check the `tasks` in parent directory, it contains `PID` of `conmon` process.
And if we check the `tasks` in sub directory, it contains `PIDs` of processes running 
inside container. This now makes sense to me as the cgroup resource limit will be 
imposed on the processes running inside container.
 
- For example, run a container with limits:
```
# podman run --cpu-shares 100 --memory 100m -d alpine sleep 10000 &
# ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220

# podman ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE    COMMAND       CREATED AT                      STATUS             PORTS   NAMES
ff67fb184d21   alpine   sleep 10000   2018-02-23 10:56:12 -0500 EST   Up 5 seconds ago           quirky_meninsky
```

- Navigate to cgroup slice directory and check `tasks`, it contains `PID` of `conmon` process:
```
# pwd
/sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220

# cat tasks
4674
4676

# ps alxwww | grep -e PID -e 4674 -e 4676
F   UID   PID  PPID PRI  NI    VSZ   RSS WCHAN  STAT TTY        TIME COMMAND
1     0  4674     1  20   0  86080  1896 SyS_po Ssl  ?          0:00 /usr/libexec/crio/conmon -c ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220 -u ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/userdata/pidfile -l /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/userdata/ctr.log --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket
4     0  4684  4674  20   0   1512     4 hrtime Ss   ?          0:00 sleep 10000
```

- Check `PID` of process running inside container:
```
# podman top quirky_meninsky
  UID   PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
    0  4684  4674  0 10:56 ?        00:00:00 sleep 10000
```

- Inside sub directory with container ID, `tasks` contains `PID` of process running inside container:
```
# pwd
/sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220

# cat tasks 
4684
```

- And the subdirectory have cgroup limits:
```
# cat memory.limit_in_bytes 
104857600

# cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/libpod-conmon-ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/ff67fb184d21d9862d4bd10e77ad6e05db61790c6085c30741b6ba1c47bf5220/cpu.shares 
100
```

- However `podman stats` does not reflect the resource limits:
```
# podman stats --no-stream
ID             NAME              CPU %   MEM USAGE / LIMIT   MEM %   NET IO    BLOCK IO   PIDS
ff67fb184d21   quirky_meninsky   --      1.212MB / 4.136GB   0.03%   -- / --   -- / --    2
```
@mrunalp Is this correct the cgroups should only apply to the container processes not the conmon process?

With `podman 0.3.2-dev`, I see the limits in `podman stats`.
```
# podman version
Version:       0.3.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.9.4
Git Commit:    "c187538b9e7fa9af75a4123ff3dc128ce6f4ba1b"
Built:         Fri Mar  2 17:12:53 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```
```
# podman run --cpu-shares 100 --memory 100m -d alpine sleep 10000 &

# podman stats --no-stream
ID             NAME                  CPU %   MEM USAGE / LIMIT   MEM %   NET IO        BLOCK IO   PIDS
055d9f3843df   gallant_nightingale   --      671.7kB / 104.9MB   0.64%   586B / 766B   -- / --    1
```
Awesome, this seems ready to close then
